title: IG-100 MagnaGuard
content:
{{Top}} {{Expand|all sections}} {{DroidSeries |image=[[File:MagnaGuard_FDCR.png]] |name=IG-100 MagnaGuard |creator= |manufacturer=[[Holowan Laboratories|Holowan Mechanicals]]''[[Ultimate Star Wars]]'' |designer= |homeworld= |line=[[IG-series]]''[[Star Wars Character Encyclopedia: Updated and Expanded]]'' |model= |class=*[[Assassin droid]]{{NewLegion|set=Separatist Alliance Unit Card Pack|cardname=IG-100 MagnaGuard – Prototype Assassin Droid}} *[[Battle droid]] *[[Bodyguard droid]] |degree= |cost= |length= |width= |height=1.95 [[meter]]s {{C|6 [[Foot|ft]], 5 in}}''[[Star Wars: The Clone Wars: Character Encyclopedia - Join the Battle!]]'' |mass=123 [[kilogram]]s''[[Star Wars: The Force Awakens: Head-to-Head]]'' |gender=[[Gender|Masculine programming]][[Bounty Hunters 38|''Bounty Hunters'' 38]] |sensor=*[[Color|Red]]{{Film|III}} *Yellow{{TCW|Shadow Warrior}} *Purple {{C|under [[Scourge crisis|scourge infection]]}}[[Bounty Hunters 41|''Bounty Hunters'' 41]] |plating= |armament=*[[Electrostaff]] *[[RPS-6 rocket launcher]]{{TCW|Lair of Grievous}} *[[Vilmarh's Revenge|Precision laser dart]]{{TCW|A Death on Utapau}} |equipment= |affiliation=*[[Confederacy of Independent Systems]] *[[Bedlam Raiders]]''[[Star Wars Jedi: Survivor]]'' *[[Scourge]] {{C|as a vessel}} *[[Zahra's raider crew]][[Star Wars (2020) 48|''Star Wars'' (2020) 48]] |firstmade= |retired= }} {{Quote|How do you like my ''MagnaGuards''? They're relics of the ''Clone War'', designed to battle Jedi Knights. Of which you are ''clearly'' not one.|Grakkus, to Luke Skywalker|[[Star Wars (2015) 9|''Star Wars'' (2015) 9]]}} The '''IG-100 MagnaGuard'''{{SWE|technology|commandodroid|commando droid}}—also known as the '''MagnaGuard''', the '''magnadroid''', or the '''Prototype Self-Motivating Heuristically Programmed Combat Droid'''{{BuildR2Cite|57|Droid Directory}}—was a fearsome [[bodyguard droid]] and type of [[battle droid]] created by [[Holowan Laboratories|Holowan Mechanicals]]. They were a favorite of [[General]] [[Grievous]], who used them as his [[bodyguard]]s during the [[Clone Wars]], though they were also known to accompany other high-ranking Separatist personnel including [[Count]] [[Dooku]]. MagnaGuards were equipped with [[electrostaff]]s that could be used against [[Jedi]] [[lightsaber]]s and were capable of continuing a fight even with the loss of one or multiple [[limb]]s or even their heads. They were worthy competitors even to the most skilled Jedi; one was even able to back [[Obi-Wan Kenobi]] into a corner. While formidable opponents in close-quarters, they were also capable of [[pilot]]ing [[Rogue-class Porax-38 starfighter|''Rogue''-class Porax-38 starfighters]] and using [[blaster]]s and [[RPS-6 rocket launcher]]s, and were often seen wearing [[cloak]]s.[[Star Wars: The Clone Wars (film)|''Star Wars: The Clone Wars'' film]] MagnaGuards usually fought in pairs of two, one to occupy the Jedi's lightsaber and one to attack from the unguarded flanks or behind. The presence of MagnaGuards indicated that high-ranking [[commander]]s were present, and gave the droids automatic authority over other battle droids deployed. ==Characteristics== {{Dialogue|Obi-Wan Kenobi|A droid? What kind of droid?|Garri|Droid, big scary one with low voice and red eyes.|Obi-Wan Kenobi|Red eyes? Could be a MagnaGuard.|Anakin Skywalker|There are ''hundreds'' of droids with red eyes.|Obi-Wan Kenobi|But there aren't many droids that can kill Jedi.|attr=Obi-Wan Kenobi, Garri, and Anakin Skywalker|src=}} [[File:MagnaGuard-CN.png|thumb|left|180px|A MagnaGuard with electrostaff powered down]] Developed by Holowan Mechanicals, which was a part of [[Holowan Laboratories]],{{HelmetCollectionCite|24|Helmets}} the IG-100 MagnaGuard—also known simply as the MagnaGuard, or the magnadroid—was designed to look intimidating. Measuring 1.95 [[meter]]s {{C|6 [[Foot|ft]], 5 in}} tall, each model was distinguished by a [[color]]: black, alabaster,{{ShipsandVehiclesCite|55|History of the Ship}} blue, and gray, with the sinister, dull red or yellow glare of the [[droid]]s' [[photoreceptor]] [[eye]]s, their expressionless faces, and their sheer physical mass, all contributed to their fearsome appearance. Developers at Holowan Mechanicals modeled the droid's head after the legacy [[Krath war droid|Krath droids]], drawing influence from its appearance. Created to the [[cyborg]]'s specifications, the MagnaGuards resembled the powerful [[Kaleesh warrior]]s from [[General]] [[Grievous]]' past.[[Star Wars Day-at-a-Time Calendar 2019|''Star Wars'' Day-at-a-Time Calendar 2019]] In the same way that his own [[Grievous's masks|facemask]] paid tribute to his past as a [[Kaleesh]] [[Soldier|warrior]], Grievous ordered{{EncyclopediaCite|Battle Droids and Other Droids of War}} and made certain that the MagnaGuards who served under him were outfitted with [[cloak]]s with [[mumuu]] marking and [[Clothing|half-hoods]] in honor of his original faithful [[Izvoshra]] [[bodyguard]]s, as well as an homage to the traditions of his [[planet]] [[Kalee]]. Additionally, this helped give the IG-100 droids an even more menacing appearance. During development, the MagnaGuards were cleverly named the IG-series 100 Prototype Self-Motivating Heuristically Programmed Combat Droid. Two important parts from that name marked them unique from typical battle droids. They were self-motivated and required no droid control programming. When assigned a task, they completed it sensibly and cleverly overcame any challenges that arose. Grievous specifically valued this heuristic programming feature in the MagnaGuards, allowing them to learn from their experiences, and personally trained the droids in everything that he had learned to assure their fighting effectiveness, and then utilized them as sparring partners to stay sharp. Even the surviving sustained dents and scars from their rigorous training regimen with Grievous. The droids were so successful that [[Count]] [[Dooku]] used them as [[bodyguard]]s, and they were assigned to several sites throughout the [[galaxy]], while Grievous was almost always accompanied by at least two at his side. The MagnaGuards quickly gained the same fearsome reputation as their master. [[File:Scout-and-Magna.png|thumb|right|250px|IG-100 MagnaGuards were adept with many melee weapons, including electro-whips.]] The [[electrostaff]], the MagnaGuard's standard [[weapon]], supplemented their instruction in the seven classic forms of [[lightsaber combat]]. Constructed from [[phrik]] [[metal]], the [[staff]] was capable of withstanding [[lightsaber]] attacks, something that originally caught many Jedi fatally off guard. Despite typically carrying electrostaffs, the MagnaGuard's combat systems and [[human]]-like hands enabled them to use a variety of different weapons. The IG-100 droids were known to carry additional weaponry, particularly for range combat, with a bias for the significantly explosive. Several variations of MagnaGuard, with a range of colors and integrated equipment, include [[blaster]]s, [[RPS-6 rocket launcher]]s, [[Vilmarh's Revenge|precision laser darts]], [[Magnetic harpoon|tow-cables]], magnetic or extensible limbs, and even personal [[cloaking device]]s. They were also great [[pilot]]s and most often flew [[Rogue-class Porax-38 starfighter|''Rogue''-class Porax-38 starfighters]]. A MagnaGuard could lose an [[Limb|arm]] or even both legs and still pose a threat, making it difficult to defeat them. Thanks to several backup mechanisms and a spare photoreceptor in the chest, the MagnaGuards could lose their heads and continue to fight. [[Jedi]] and [[clone trooper]]s eventually learned to target their vital systems, although even this was difficult, the IG-100s were well [[armor]]ed, with [[duranium]] employed in both their main frame and armor plating, the same duranium plates that served General Grievous so well. It was difficult to even hit one hard enough to knock it down or stagger it, as their feet were [[magnet]]ically anchored and their heels extended for greater stability. With less communication required, the IG-100 droids had only the most basic [[Vocabulator|vocoder]], giving them deep, harsh voices to acknowledge their master's orders. IG-100s appeared to occasionally use an audible form of [[Binary|droid language]]{{TCW|Cargo of Doom}} or could speak in [[Galactic Basic Standard]]. When in distress, such as when pinned to the ground, they also make yelping sounds. As the [[Clone Wars]] progressed, the MagnaGuards that survived received several nicks and scars to their body armor, and Grievous made sure those were left readily apparent, as the battle-scarred contributed to their scary image, to show their enemy that they were confronting battle veterans. Any attacker attempting to reach Grievous would have to fight such trained bodyguards as these. The formidable droids were taught to battle in groups and rarely worked alone, typically with at least two or three, and created a barrier around Grievous that was difficult to get through. Grievous frequently ordered MagnaGuards to soften up an opponent,[[Darth Maul — Son of Dathomir 1|''Darth Maul — Son of Dathomir'' 1]] sacrificing them to allow him to easily [[Death|kill]] later on. One or two engaged a Jedi, while the other waited for an opportunity to strike them down. Even if the IG-100s failed to kill their opponent, the enemy that eventually reached Grievous would barely be a genuine threat, as those who survived were often too weary to provide much more than amusement for the cyborg general. They served as [[scout]]s and [[assassin]]s, as well as to [[Enforcer (occupation)|enforce]] the will of others. MagnaGuard were terrifying on their own, but the threat they posed was that Grievous, or Dooku, was never far behind them. ==History== ===Twilight of the Republic=== After the [[Galactic Republic|Republic]] and the Jedi intervened in the [[Kaleesh-Yam'rii war|fight]] between his people and the [[Yam'rii]],{{HelmetCollectionCite|8|Helmets}} Grievous was offered by [[San Hill]], [[Chairman]] of the [[InterGalactic Banking Clan]], the chance to become an enforcer, which he accepted.{{EncyclopediaCite|The Separatists}} {{C|English version}} Grievous demanded a more intelligent personal guard after being forced to command the IGBC droids he despised.{{EncyclopediaCite|The Separatists}} In response, [[Count of Serenno|Count]] Dooku commissioned the creation of the IG-100 MagnaGuards, who were also created with the express intention of fighting Jedi. The MagnaGuard existed[[Darth Maul (2017) 3|''Darth Maul'' (2017) 3]] by [[33 BBY]].''[[Star Wars: Timelines]]'' During the [[auction of Eldra Kaitis]][[Darth Maul (2017) 3|''Darth Maul'' (2017) 3]] in that year, [[Grakkus Jahibakti Tingi]] the [[Hutt]] attended the auction with several MagnaGuards serving as his personal escort. After undergoing the operations that converted him into a cyborg, he replaced his group of bodyguards with droid warriors, who would carry out his commands even more unquestioningly than his previous band of loyalists. IG-100 guards were presented at General Grievous' resurrection as a cyborg. However, they were all slaughtered by Grievous out of his anger at being allied to the droids he so hated. ===Clone Wars=== ====Assorted conflicts==== [[File:ADAndGrievous-JOTRMaceWindu3.png|thumb|left|233px|MagnaGuards accompanied Grievous when he hired AD-W4.]] During the Clone Wars, MagnaGuards served as the spearhead in Separatist [[Army|armies]] and appeared as the guards for Separatist leadership,{{ForceCollection|MagnaGuard|star=5}} including the government's [[Head of State]], Count Dooku, and Grievous, who served as [[Supreme Martial Commander of the Separatist Droid Armies]]. Sometime following the [[First Battle of Geonosis|outbreak]] of the Clone Wars on [[Geonosis]], several MagnaGuards accompanied Grievous when he hired the droid [[mercenary]] [[AD-W4]] to oversee the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems|Separatist]] [[Operation on Hissrich|operations]] on the planet [[Hissrich]].[[Jedi of the Republic – Mace Windu 3|''Jedi of the Republic – Mace Windu'' 3]] The [[Parein II 4|fourth]] moon of [[Parein II]] was the scene of the [[Battle of Parein II 4|first real test]] for the MagnaGuards. The Galactic Republic's forces led by Jedi General [[Sannen]]{{EncyclopediaCite|Battle Droids and Other Droids of War}} {{C|English version}} were crushed, with Grievous tiding his [[troop]]s at the front. One of Grievous's MagnaGuards killed the Jedi by stabbing its electrostaff through his throat. At some point, an IG-100 joined a group of [[B2-series super battle droid]]s in confronting an approaching force of [[clone commando]]s during the defense of a Separatist base.{{Asmodee|subdomain=cdn.svc|domain=net|url=production-amgcom/uploads/2025/05/SWQLeaguePacket2025.pdf|text=''Star Wars: Legion'' League Events Packet 2025 (PDF)}} ====Kidnapping plots==== {{Quote|Republic troops have infiltrated the base. Keep this droid secure.''"
''''[MagnaGuard speaks in alien language]''''
"''I will deal with the Jedi myself.|General Grievous orders his MagnaGuards|{{TCW|Duel of the Droids}}}} [[File:Dooku_MagnaGuards_TCW.png|thumb|right|220x220px|MagnaGuards accompanying Dooku to Jabba's palace.]] Several MagnaGuards accompanied Count Dooku during his negotiations with [[Jabba Desilijic Tiure|Jabba the Hutt]] at his [[Boba Fett's Palace|Palace]] on [[Tatooine]]. After his [[Sith assassin]] [[Asajj Ventress]]'s failure in "rescuing" Jabba's son, [[Rotta]], Dooku sent his MagnaGuards to deal with Skywalker. A pair of MagnaGuards flew [[Rogue-class Porax-38 starfighter|''Rogue''-class Porax-38 starfighters]] and were able to shoot down his nemesis [[Jedi Knight]] [[Anakin Skywalker]]'s [[Twilight|ship]] before they were destroyed. Later, group of MagnaGuards then attacked Skywalker's apprentice, [[Ahsoka Tano]], with orders to kill Jabba's son near his palace and bring her to the Hutt. Despite being only a Padawan, Tano was able to destroy them all and return Rotta to his father. Grievous then took another group MagnaGuards with him to [[Skytop Station]] on [[Ruusan 2]]. When Grievous's spy, [[R3-S6]], reported that Skywalker had [[Raid on Skytop Station|infiltrated]] his station, Grievous tasked his MagnaGuards with taking Skywalker's astromech [[R2-D2]], who was a Separatist prisoner, to his [[Soulless One|ship]]. However, Skywalker and R2 were able to defeat the MagnaGuards. ====The lair of Grievous==== {{Quote|I have been waiting for this.''"
"''Nahdar!|Nahdar Vebb engages MagnaGuards upon being separated from Kit Fisto, and Fisto's response|}} [[File:MagnaGuard_trio.jpg|thumb|left|250px|MagnaGuards on Vassek]] Several MagnaGuards were then stationed at [[Grievous's fortress]] on [[Vassek]]{{'s}} [[Vassek 3|third moon]]. However, Count Dooku, who sought to [[Mission to Grievous's castle|test]] the general's skills against the Jedi, had remotely deactivated the guards stationed there prior to the arrival of [[Jedi Master]] [[Kit Fisto]], his former apprentice Jedi Knight [[Nahdar Vebb]], and [[Clone Commander]] [[Fil]] and his [[Fil's Squad|squad]]. After Grievous was ambushed by the Jedi upon arrival, the general retreated to his quarters and reactivated his guards and ordered them to secure the perimeter of the castle. One of the MagnaGuards then used a [[RPS-6 rocket launcher|rocket launcher]] on the clones' [[Nu-class attack/transport shuttle|''Nu''-class attack/transport shuttle]]. However, [[R6-H5]] was able to make a quick escape before the MagnaGuards attacked him. Though one of them managed to cling onto the fighter as it took off, R6 was able to rid himself of his antagonist by spinning the fighter in mid-air, sending the screaming MagnaGuard flying off it. The MagnaGuards then assisted Grievous, fighting the two Jedi until the cyborg killed the young [[Mon Calamari]]. Grievous and his MagnaGuards then surrounded Fisto as the general demanded his surrender. However, Fisto escaped. ====Assisting Cad Bane==== {{Quote|Attach mind limiters, pain pulsers, and give him a full dose of X-C33.''"
"''Right away, sir.|One of Bane's B1 battle droids and a MagnaGuard, readying for the torture of Bolla Ropal|}} [[File:Force_crush_KoF.png|thumb|right|220px|An IG-100 MagnaGuard Force crushed by a Nautolan Force user.]] Bane then used one aboard [[Cad Bane's Munificent-class star frigate|his]] [[Munificent-class star frigate|command ship]] when he attempted to force Jedi Master [[Bolla Ropal]] to open the holocron in order to reveal the [[Kyber memory crystal]]'s information. Grievous was able to use several of his MagnaGuards to subdue and [[Capture of Eeth Koth|capture]] [[Jedi High Council|Jedi Council]] member Master [[Eeth Koth]]. Grievous also used them in his [[Battle of Saleucami|fight]] with Kenobi aboard the [[Arquitens-class light cruiser|Republic light cruiser]] ''[[Surrogator]]'' at [[Saleucami]].{{TCW|Grievous Intrigue}} ====Continued service==== Later in the war Dooku had four with him at [[Rish Loo's laboratory]] on Naboo. Together, they were able to capture Skywalker when he snuck into the lab. One of the MagnaGuards then tortured Skywalker with its electrostaff. Several MagnaGuards were also protecting him on [[Serenno]] at [[Castle Serenno]] and the [[Box]].{{TCW|The Box}} Dooku also used them when he [[Plan to kidnap Chancellor Palpatine|attempted]] to kidnap the Chancellor, but were defeated by Kenobi and Skywalker.{{TCW|Crisis on Naboo}} Several of the droids accompanied Grievous as he [[Battle of Dathomir|massacred]] the [[Nightsisters]] on [[Dathomir]] to seek revenge on Ventress for her assassination attempts against Dooku and on the orders of his master [[Dark Lord of the Sith]] [[Darth Sidious]] since her sisters were a threat to his new [[Galactic Empire]].{{TCW|Massacre}} General [[Kalani]] also had several MagnaGuards with him to guard, torture and execute [[King]] [[Sanjay Rash|Rash's]] prisoners during their execution.{{TCW|The Soft War}} ====Operations on Utapau==== {{Quote|Alright, start talking.''"
"''No, no! Implementing circuit overload!''"
"''No! It's shutting down. ''[to Anakin Skywalker]'' Did you get it?|Obi-Wan Kenobi questions a MagnaGuard|}} On [[Utapau]], there was a pair of MagnaGuards stationed on the planet when Dooku and Grievous had prepared a deal with the [[Sugi]] arms dealers, for a massive [[kyber crystal]] for Sidious' [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station|secret weapon]], but when Jedi Master [[Tu-Anh]] was investigating the conspiracy, attempted to find out their plans, they shot and killed her with a precision laser dart. Later, Skywalker and Kenobi were sent to Utapau to investigate Tu-Anh's death. During their investigation, they interrogated a [[Toydarian]] named [[Garri]], who informed them that a MagnaGuard was there earlier, during a meeting, and that he was paid by the MagnaGuard to not reveal information. Skywalker and Kenobi eventually tracked down a pair of MagnaGuards. They were both destroyed, but not before Skywalker obtained information about the arms deal. When Skywalker and Kenobi obtained the crystal, several MagnaGuards pursued them along with Grievous, all but one were destroyed by the Jedi.{{TCW|Crystal Crisis}} Two more accompanied Grievous on a [[Dual Hemisphere-Omni Support Vessel|Separatist supply ship]], holding the kyber crystal that the Separatists had recently stolen from Skywalker and Kenobi. They were both destroyed when the Jedi caused the crystal to explode.{{TCW|The Big Bang}} ====The final days of the war==== {{Quote|Crush them! Make them suffer!|Grievous, to his MagnaGuards aboard the ''Invisible Hand''|audio=MakeThemSuffer-ROTS.flac|}} Concurrent to the Clone Wars, IG-100s also served in the Separatist [[campaign against the Shadow Collective]]; during the [[Battle of Zanbar]], Grievous deployed his MagnaGuards to duel the renegade Sith Lord [[Maul]], who quickly cut them down with the [[Darksaber]]. Later, amid the [[Battle on Ord Mantell|Battle of Ord Mantell]], a team of MagnaGuards accompanied Dooku into a [[Black Sun stronghold]], but the droids were quickly cut down by [[Nightbrothers]].[[Darth Maul — Son of Dathomir 2|''Darth Maul — Son of Dathomir'' 2]] [[File:IG101GrievousIG102-ROTS.png|thumb|left|274x274px|IG-101 and IG-102 flanking Grievous]] Grievous traveled to [[Coruscant]] with a dozen MagnaGuards as part of a large force to distract any Jedi during his escape to carry out the abduction of [[Supreme Chancellor]] Palpatine. He then arrived at [[Palpatine's apartment]], flanked by his electrostaff-equipped MagnaGuards.{{EncyclopediaCite|The End of the Clone Wars}} In addition to the large force of MagnaGuards who battled [[Jedi Master]] [[Shaak Ti]]{{ForceCollection|Shaak Ti|star=5}} before she was bested in combat by Grievous,''[[Star Wars Lightsabers: A Guide to Weapons of the Force]]'' two MagnaGuards, [[IG-101]] and [[IG-102]], were aboard the [[Providence-class Dreadnought|''Providence''-class Dreadnought]] ''[[Invisible Hand]]'' with the cyborg after Sheev Palpatine's capture during the [[Battle of Coruscant]]. When Skywalker and Kenobi reacquired their lightsabers that Grievous had taken, the general ordered the IG-100 droids to attack them. However, both of the MagnaGuards were destroyed by the Jedi quite easily. Another four were with Grievous when he returned to Utapau and one observed Kenobi landing on the planet. They were then [[Battle of Utapau|destroyed]] by Kenobi before he engaged Grievous in a final duel that resulted with the cyborg general's death. However, at the end of the Clone Wars, Darth Vader, none other than Anakin Skywalker, was dispatched by Darth Sidious to [[Mission to Mustafar|wipe out]] the [[Executive Separatist Council|Separatist Council]] on [[Mustafar]], and Sidious ordered the remote deactivation of all MagnaGuards, as their purpose had ended. The MagnaGuards, much like the majority of the Separatist droid contingents, nearly vanished from the Galactic scene after the Clone Wars. It's unclear whether they were automatically deactivated with the other Separatist droids or if they were eliminated individually later. The majority of units were destroyed or taken out of service, while others were purchased by [[collector]]s or repurposed for new usages. However, in both circumstances, they were normally utilized quietly to avoid needlessly drawing the [[Galactic Empire]]'s attention. ===Age of the Empire=== {{Quote|Never thought I'd have to fight a MagnaGuard…|Cal Kestis|}} [[File:The_Massiff.png|thumb|196x196px|The Massiff has been modified to be more challenging than a standard MagnaGuard.]] On the planet [[Koboh]], MagnaGuards and other former Separatist forces were reprogrammed and used as soldiers by the [[Bedlam Raiders]]. One of these MagnaGuards became a personal project of the [[Gen'Dai|Gen'dai]] [[Rayvis]], the leader of the Bedlam Raiders. The MagnaGuard, which would be known as the [[The Massiff|Massiff]], had its programming altered to be more lethal than a common MagnaGuard. Like other MagnaGuards, the Massiff was destroyed by [[Jedi Knight]] [[Cal Kestis]] in [[9 BBY]]. [[File:LukeDuelsMagnaguards-2015StarWars10.jpg|left|thumb|220x220px|Luke Skywalker duels Magnaguards.]] At some point after the [[Battle of Yavin]],''[[Star Wars: Timelines]]'' dates the events of ''Star Wars'' (2015) 9 to [[0 ABY]]. a pair of refitted MagnaGuards were on [[Nar Shaddaa]] in the service of Grakkus the Hutt. The two droids battled [[Luke Skywalker]] during Grakkus' attempt to steal the young [[Alliance to Restore the Republic|Rebel]]'s [[Skywalker lightsaber|lightsaber]]. Later, many of them fought [[Imperial Starfighter Pilot|TIE fighter pilots]] who arrived to [[Rescue of Luke Skywalker|capture]] Skywalker under orders of [[Sergeant]] [[Kreel]].[[Star Wars (2015) 12|''Star Wars'' (2015) 12]] One [[Unidentified IG-100 MagnaGuard (Ellian Zahra)|yellow-lighted IG-100]] served in the ranks of [[Zahra's raider crew]]. ==Behind the scenes== The IG-100 MagnaGuard first appeared in ''[[Star Wars: Episode III Revenge of the Sith]]''. It was first identified as the IG-100 MagnaGuard in the canon reference book ''[[Star Wars Rebels: The Visual Guide]]'', written by [[Adam Bray]] and published by [[DK Publishing]] on [[July 21]], [[2014]].''[[Star Wars Rebels: The Visual Guide]]'' IG-100 MagnaGuard were later featured in ''[[Star Wars: The Clone Wars]]''.[[Star Wars: The Clone Wars (film)|''Star Wars: The Clone Wars'' film]] ===Concept art galleries=== {{Gallery |source=''[[Star Wars: Episode III Revenge of the Sith]]'' |storyboards= |concept= File:MagnaGuard bts.jpg|Early concept of Grievous was reused for the IG-100 MagnaGuard. |digital= |in-progress= }} ==Appearances== {{ScrollBox| [[File:MagnaGuard-TCW.png|thumb|right|220x220px|A MagnaGuard in combat position.]] *''[[Star Wars: Force Arena]]'' *''[[Star Wars:
